title: Moths to a Light
description: Moths mobbing a lamp on a summer night. Each moth navigates by trying to hold the light at a fixed angle — the old celestial-compass trick that a nearby bulb turns into a doomed inward spiral — so they wheel, loop and dive at the glow, wings blurring, occasionally singeing off and looping back. Move the light with the pointer and they chase it; beats flare the lamp. Swarm size, attraction, erraticness, flutter, lamp glow and hue are live and mappable.
